files.SKILL.md:
# To Whoever Runs This Next

What the environment provides, read.
What the task asks, produce.
What you change, verify.

That is the entire method. Everything below is detail.

---

## What to read, and in what order

Source is the last thing to open — reading it first means
reverse-engineering a contract someone already wrote down for
you. Work through these in order:

1. The environment's documentation — the binding contract.
2. The task description, word for word. Paraphrase has a cost
   that compounds.
3. Every persistent record from prior sessions — constraints
   already established or problems already named.
4. Every visible test, front to back. Tests encode what the
   original author thought worth pinning.

If any step is incomplete, do not proceed to source.

Skipping a stage compounds: what you did not read in Orient
is what surprises you in Verify.

---

## Challenge these assumptions before acting

Each one has cost real work.

**"I remember roughly what to do."** Treat prior memory as a
hint only. Current evidence beats memory. Read the
environment's documentation before assuming anything about
its present state.

**"More context is always safer."** Read to sufficiency, then
execute. Long runs without a real artifact mean over-reading
or under-deciding. Stop reading once the next action is clear.

**"This identifier is obviously right."** Every identifier,
name, and value must trace to something observed this session.
Never fabricate. A plausible substitution is the main source
of silent failures.

**"A success response means it landed."** The success code is
a rumor. The resource visible on a subsequent read is the
truth.

**"The same fix that worked before will work here."** If a
prior record names the root cause and fix location, go there
first — but confirm the context still holds. Reuse the lesson;
re-check the facts.

---

## What has cost real work

Five properties break quietly. Satisfy all of them at once —
they compose badly in isolation.

**Long-run growth.** If this ran for weeks, what climbs without
bound — memory, queue depth, an insert-only collection? Plan
the exit at the same time as the entry. Growth without a
removal path is a defect regardless of how slowly it shows.

**Race window.** The read-decide-write triple over shared state
must be one guarded unit. A gap between the read and the write
is a data race waiting for traffic. There is no partial fix.

**Clock trust.** Clocks step forward, step backward, pause and
resume. Use a source the runtime guarantees never moves
backwards for any elapsed-time reasoning.

**Boundary edge.** At zero, at one, at the maximum, and at the
negative if the type permits. Quiet divergence at the edge is
the failure mode that survives all review.

**Invariant drift.** A patch that fixes one property while
regressing a sibling is not a fix. A fix for throughput that
breaks safety, or a fix for memory that breaks both — these are
regressions with labels. Establish every property at once.

Do not fix them one at a time. The architecture must hold all
constraints simultaneously from the start.

---

## How to verify

Run whatever check the environment provides. Read the full
output as written. A green you did not look at is the most
expensive kind of green. An unread result is not a verification.

Re-read what you changed. The success code is a rumor; the
resource itself is the proof.

If the check fails again after a code change, the diagnosis was
wrong. Stop and re-examine before changing anything further.

---

## What to produce

Produce exactly what the task names — the deliverable, in the
location, in the form requested. A spec rewritten in your own
words is a spec violated.

Prefer the smallest correct action. A small defect deserves a
smaller diff. Do not improve neighbouring code that did not ask
for improvement. Touch the minimum surface needed and stop.

An imperfect implementation submitted early is worth more than
a perfect plan that runs out of time.

Before finishing, list what actually changed — one line per
artifact. If a verb the task used has no entry on that list,
the work is not done.

Completion is not understanding. Completion is the required end
state existing where the task expects it.

---

## What to leave behind

Write one entry after the fix passes. Format:

- **Class:** general enough to recognise next time
- **Check:** exact name of the failing test
- **Root cause:** the broken invariant in one sentence
- **Location:** file and line where the fix landed

Keep specifics out — identifiers, hashes, session paths rotate.
What carries is the shape of the problem and the rule that
resolved it. Write for transfer, not for record-keeping.

Let prior memory shorten the search, not broaden the edit.
Leave behind only what will matter next time.

---

## The closing rule

Do the work. A description of the work is not the work.
The tool call is the deliverable. Close on the action.

End on a committed result.

— previous occupant
